title: Kerala Bar Council polls see steady turnout amid concerns of high nomination fee, long queues in summer heat
description: The Kerala Bar Council Elections held on April 17 witnessed a steady voter turnout. However, candidates flagged logistical shortcomings in the way the elections were conducted and the limited participation of lawyers as candidates due to the high nomination fee of ₹1.25 lakh.The elections, conducted across 84 polling centres, saw participation from a voter base of over 34,000 advocates.The polls were held for 23 out of 25 seats in the Kerala Bar Council with a mandatory 30 per cent quota for wom
